onboarding.question: Clarify the concrete observable behavior that makes the session expiry warning non-dismissible — specify whether it has no close control, cannot be swiped, and when exactly it clears (e.g., only on user action that resets the inactivity timer).
onboarding.recommendations:
[1]. My recommendation: The session expiry warning banner (AC#8) has no close button, close affordance, or swipe-to-dismiss gesture; it is a fixed banner at the top of the chat interface that cannot be manually dismissed or removed by the user; it clears automatically only when the user performs an action that resets the inactivity timer (e.g., sends a message, taps a button, or types in the prompt bar); if the session expires while the banner is visible, the banner is immediately replaced by the session-expired inline error message.

→ Rationale: A truly non-dismissible banner without close affordances ensures the user cannot accidentally ignore a critical warning; automatic clearing on user action respects the principle that action resets the timer and the warning is no longer needed; this definition is concrete and implementable.

→ Note: Pending your approval — say yes to apply to the product spec

---
